source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) pdf/A-TCF-5-159 Usability Verification and Validation/A-TCF-5-108 Usability Engineering Report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: This document specifies a process for H&abyz Co., Ltd. to analyse, specify, design, verify and validate usability as it relates to safety of the medical device.
keyPoints: The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, and is not to be used for mammography.
Applicable standards are EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010.
The usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors during normal use.
All usage scenarios identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.
Usability verification result for UI-01 through UI-07 is listed as No issues and Pass.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This document specifies a process for a manufacturer, H&abyz Co., Ltd.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e., normal use.

concepts.name: Usability engineering file
concepts.evidence: This document is the usability engineering file of H&abyz Co., Ltd.

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: Summative evaluation user interface evaluation conducted at the end of the user interface development with the intent to obtain objective evidence that the user interface can be used safely

concepts.name: User Interface of Unknown Provenance
concepts.evidence: UOUP User Interface Of Unknown Provenance user interface or part of a user interface of a medical device previously developed for which adequate records of the usability engineering process of this standard are not available

concepts.name: Risk control related to user interface design
concepts.evidence: To reduce use-related risk, the manufacturer shall use one or more of the following options, in the priority listed (as required by ISO 14971:2019, 7.1)

evidence.claim: The document is A-TCF-5-108, revision 0, titled Usability Engineering Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document. No. A-TCF-5-108 Rev. No. 0 Usability Engineering Report

evidence.claim: The report revision date is Dec 22, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Report Rev. date Dec 22, 2022

evidence.claim: The product covered is the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, model names A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The covered device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW (Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector) is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adults and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: Applicable standards listed in the document overview are EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010.
Normative references include EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Normative references Standard No. Standard Title EN 60601-1-6:2010 ... EN 62366-1:2015 ... IEC TR 62366-2:2016 ... EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adults and children, except infants, with pregnant women excluded.
evidence.evidenceText: Age group Adult and Children (except infants) ... Patient group/state except pregnant women
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Patient Population

evidence.claim: The intended user profile includes radiologists who can read and understand the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Knowledge User must be read and understand user manual. ... Experience Radiologist
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended User

evidence.claim: The use environment is an X-ray room with operating temperature 10℃ to 35℃, humidity 20 to 75% RH, and air pressure 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Environment ... Temperature Operation condition: 10℃ to 35℃ ... Humidity Operation condition: 20 to 75% relative humidity ... Air Pressure Operation condition: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa ... Place X-ray room
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Use Environment

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include checking the user manual, preparing for use, product use, and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions 1 Check the User manual 2 Preparation for using the product 3 product use 4 Maintenance
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Use Scenarios

evidence.claim: Foreseeable hazardous situations include no reading, inappropriate image result, electric energy, failure of function, and failure of electronic with electric shock.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreseeable hazards and hazardous situations were identified ... no reading ... inappropriate image result ... Electric energy ... failure of function ... failure of electronic and electric shock
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Hazard Analysis

evidence.claim: Possible harms identified include delayed diagnosis of patients, misdiagnosis, electric shock, and burn.
evidence.evidenceText: Harm ... Delayed diagnosis of patients ... misdiagnosis ... Electric shock ... burn
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Hazard Analysis

evidence.claim: All usage scenarios identified in section 5.4 were selected for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: Usability verification items UI-01 through UI-07 passed with no issues.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Verification Result UI-01 No issues Pass ... UI-07 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report A-TCF-5-107.
evidence.evidenceText: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-107).

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance is not applicable to the covered device.
evidence.evidenceText: However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW).

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal lists FDA, but the document text does not substantiate FDA authority.
The source is marked confidential and appears to be manufacturer technical documentation rather than an authority-issued regulatory document.
Text contains minor OCR or drafting artifacts such as repeated headers, grammatical errors, and 'date cable' where context suggests data cable.
